references.source: NHS digital service manual accessibility content guidance
references.borrowed: Clear headings, specific control names, and non-color-only status communication.
references.rejected: Did not copy NHS-branded component chrome because the staff workspace shell owns local product styling.

references.borrowed: Native button disclosure with aria-expanded, aria-controls, and Enter/Space behavior.
references.rejected: No custom disclosure role because a native button is sufficient.

references.borrowed: Dialog guidance helped decide not to present the provenance drawer as modal because the rest of the shell is not inert.
references.rejected: No modal dialog or focus trap for provenance detail.

references.borrowed: Locator-scoped ARIA snapshot assertions for compact and expanded confidence states.
references.rejected: No full-page ARIA baseline because unrelated workspace chrome changes frequently.

references.borrowed: Isolated browser contexts per fixture to avoid route and local storage leakage.
references.rejected: No shared page state between confidence fixtures.

references.borrowed: Element-level screenshot baselines for the assistive rail and confidence surface.
references.rejected: No page-level visual baseline because the queue and task canvas are outside task 420 scope.

references.borrowed: Dense work surfaces should keep actions discoverable through consistent visible controls and keyboard patterns.
references.rejected: No command-menu shortcut layer because disclosure controls need direct proximity to the artifact.

references.borrowed: Progressive disclosure can compress dense explanatory content when section titles give enough overview.
references.rejected: No multi-accordion component because task 420 needs one bounded rationale and one source lineage reveal.

references.borrowed: Factor rows use condensed structured labels and values for scan speed.
references.rejected: No selectable structured list behavior because confidence factors are read-only.

references.borrowed: Persistent layout guidance supports keeping the assistive rail mounted while route-specific content changes.
references.rejected: No Next.js implementation detail because this app uses Vite; only the shell continuity principle applies.
